specific_location: Jackson Reservoir, last seen on NW shore at very, very close range
description: Large bull-necked jaeger species baiscally dark brown throughout but with extensive white at the base of the primaries and rectrices.; underwing and axillaries were barred. The back and rump showed some barring; thick, black tipped gray bill and gray legs; dark eye.  The central rectrices showed a stubby extension.  The bird was large enough to take a Franklin's Gull out of the sky, kill it and consume it.Behaviors: The bird alternated between sitting on the water and foraging past other birds, occasionally giving chase to smaller gulls.  The bird was large enough to take a Franklin's Gull out of the sky, kill it and consume it.Call: nonePlumage: probable sub adult dark or barred phase
photographer: Photographer: Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes
